IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: This is the planned power cut that was previously scheduled for the 28th of April 2026 but was cancelled on the day due to emergency gas works. To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. By carefully planning this work in advance, we have been able to minimise the duration of the power interruption. This essential work will cover approximately 250 metres of tree cutting across 5 sections of the overhead network.This work is taking place on London Road.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 20:09 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We know the time frame we estimated is now approaching. Our engineers are continuing their work to repair our overhead network in the Manningtree area.

As our staff are working with live electricity, we need to wait until it is safe for them to stop and contact us with an update.

We're sorry you've been impacted by this, and we'll provide you an update as soon as possible. Sent by Simon at 16:18.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 15:26 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 18:21 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 22:48 - Thanks for bearing with us tonight.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 12:25 - We can now confirm that all power in the Nathans Lane area should be back on. After investigation, our engineers have now restarted a temporary generator which had been unexpectedly stopped.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut today, our staff will work to restore your power as quickly and as safely as possible. A lot of our customers have questions regarding power cuts, and though you can always reach our team, you can also find some helpful information and answers to some of our most frequently asked questions on our website here - https://UKPN.short.gy/FAQ - Bear in mind this will use your mobile data, so feel free to speak to us if you'd prefer. We'll be in touch again once we have more information from our staff.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Luke at 09:35 - We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.
